intent: Fully remove the unusable focus-mode daemon (Steam/browser mutual exclusion) and every reference to it, since it no longer works and the user asked for permanent removal.
scope: Non-goals: phone_focus_mode/ (unrelated GPS/Android component), historical docs (SECURITY_HARDENING_ANALYSIS.md, docs/superpowers/evidence|contracts) left untouched as append-only history
changes: Deleted focus_mode_daemon.py, _focus_mode_lib.py, install_focus_mode_daemon.sh, __init__.py, and stale __pycache__ entries
Removed the ask_install "Focus-mode daemon" step and its header-comment entry from install_core_system.sh
Removed FOCUS_MODE_SCRIPT var, check_focus_mode() function, its service-list entry, and its call from check_and_enable_services.sh; renumbered the help-text service list
Removed the FOCUS MODE DAEMON TESTS block (tests 20-22) from test_security_hardening.sh; renumbered remaining test comments for consistency
Removed focus-mode mentions from CLAUDE.md's directory layout and Key scripts list

verification.command: systemctl --user is-active focus-mode.service; systemctl --user is-enabled focus-mode.service; ls /usr/local/bin/focus-mode-daemon; ls ~/.local/state/focus-mode
verification.evidence: inactive / not-found / No such file (both) — confirms nothing was live and cleanup is complete
Zero hits outside unrelated phone_focus_mode/ and untouched historical docs
Both scripts parse cleanly after edits
Runs to completion; summary table has no focus_mode row; only pre-existing unrelated warnings (hosts, workout_locker) remain
Help text no longer mentions Focus-mode daemon; secondary module list ends cleanly at item 7

16 passed / 3 failed / 5 skipped — no FOCUS MODE DAEMON section present; the 3 failures (hosts-guard.path, hosts-bind-mount.service, shutdown-schedule-guard.path not running) and 5 skips (screen_locker extracted to its own repo) are pre-existing and unrelated to this change
All hooks passed: shellcheck, codespell, AI evidence check, workflow contract check, no leaked secrets, etc.

risks: check_and_enable_services.sh renumbered its help-text service list (7->6 ... 12->11) — cosmetic only, no external script parses these numbers
test_security_hardening.sh test comments renumbered (23-27 -> 20-24) — cosmetic only, test_result() tracks by name/counter not by comment number
rollback: git revert the commit that removes the focus-mode daemon
After rollback, verify focus_mode_daemon.py and its installer are restored and check_and_enable_services.sh --status shows a focus_mode row again
